cost_assumptions.model: governance-only research cost model with nonzero synthetic spread assumption
cost_assumptions.scope: costs are assumptions for planning only, not execution evidence

data_assumptions.coverage: daily synthetic close and volume fields are assumed complete
data_assumptions.source: synthetic fixture bars only, no real market data

exclusion_rules: exclude instruments with missing synthetic close or volume fields
exclude rows whose synthetic timestamp is outside the declared fixture window
expected_failure_modes: synthetic input coverage may be too small to support later diagnostics
apparent relationship may disappear when future leakage checks are applied
promotion_criteria: future study must satisfy declared diagnostics before any evidence status changes
future review must reject unsupported alpha or tradability claims

timestamp_assumptions.availability: features are available only after the synthetic bar timestamp closes
timestamp_assumptions.timezone: timestamps use UTC and retain explicit timezone information

availability_assumptions.timing: synthetic inputs are available after the fixture bar closes

hypothesis_card.author: codex-fixture
hypothesis_card.expected_mechanism: The proposed mechanism is that synthetic volume metadata is evaluated only after declared timestamp availability rules.
falsification_criteria: Reject the record if leakage checks find that any required input is unavailable at the declared decision time.
Block the record if required negative controls are missing or fail to catch their injected faults.
hypothesis_card.rationale: Synthetic volume availability can document a research premise before any implementation work is considered.
risks: Synthetic fixtures may be too small to represent later research constraints.
Timestamp assumptions may be incomplete when future governance objects add stricter checks.
hypothesis_card.title: Synthetic volume availability lifecycle

cost_model.fees: synthetic fixed fee assumption only, not execution evidence
cost_model.spread: synthetic nonzero spread assumption for protocol metadata

path_rules.entry_time: synthetic bar close at fixture timestamp
path_rules.exit_time: five synthetic sessions after entry timestamp
path_rules.path_dependency: metadata only path rule; no labels are materialized

target_stop_rules.stop: metadata-only stop rule; no target or stop values are computed
target_stop_rules.target: metadata-only target definition for future label construction

cost_assumptions.commission: synthetic nonzero commission assumption for diagnostics planning only
cost_assumptions.slippage: synthetic nonzero slippage assumption; no execution evidence is produced

dataset_scope.source: tiny synthetic governance fixture metadata, not real market data

locked_test_policy.allowed_touch: locked test may not be inspected during diagnostics planning
locked_test_policy.contamination_handling: any later locked-test contact must be recorded as contamination metadata
locked_test_policy.oos_reuse: out-of-sample reuse is prohibited unless a later ledger records it explicitly

split_protocol.locked_test: final synthetic fixture interval reserved and untouched by diagnostics planning
split_protocol.train: first synthetic fixture interval for fitting-free diagnostics metadata
split_protocol.validation: middle synthetic fixture interval for diagnostics metadata

stopping_rules: stop when observed variants reach the declared variant budget
stop when a required negative control is missing from the future diagnostics plan
stop if locked-test contact occurs without contamination metadata

trial_records.failure_reason: synthetic dependency check failed before metrics existed
